  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go", "symbol": "RoleType"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/org/model.go", "symbol": "OrgUser"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go", "symbol": "DeclareFixedRoles"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go", "symbol": "registerDashboardRoles"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go", "symbol": "registerFolderRoles"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go", "symbol": "Middleware"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go", "symbol": "GetInheritedScopes"}
  ],
  "text": "Grafana implements a comprehensive three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) with role-based access control and folder/dashboard permission inheritance. The system is defined across multiple key files:\n\n## Core Role Definitions\n\nThe role hierarchy is defined in `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` with four role constants: RoleNone, RoleViewer, RoleEditor, and RoleAdmin. These roles have a precedence hierarchy (lines 18-23) where Admin > Editor > Viewer > None. The `pkg/services/org/model.go` defines the OrgUser model that associates users with these roles within organizations.\n\n## Permission Mapping and Enforcement\n\nRole-to-permission mapping is established in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` through the `DeclareFixedRoles` function (line 317), which grants specific fixed roles to each basic role. \n\nDashboard permissions are defined in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`:\n- DashboardViewActions (line 34) includes: dashboards:read, annotations:read\n- DashboardEditActions (line 35) adds: dashboards:write, dashboards:delete, annotations:write/delete/create\n- DashboardAdminActions (line 36) adds: dashboards.permissions:read and dashboards.permissions:write\n\nFolder permissions are defined in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`:\n- FolderViewActions (line 31) includes: folders:read, alerting:rules:read, library-panels:read, alerting:silences:read\n- FolderEditActions (line 32-44) adds write and delete actions plus dashboard creation and alert management\n- FolderAdminActions (line 45) adds: folders.permissions:read and folders.permissions:write\n\nEach role tier gets granted to the corresponding basic role through registerDashboardRoles (lines 43-79) and registerFolderRoles (lines 52-88), using the `Grants` field to specify which org roles receive each permission set.\n\n## Permission Guard Middleware\n\nThe permission enforcement middleware is implemented in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`. The `Middleware` function (line 30) creates a handler that evaluates permissions using the `Evaluator` interface. The `authorize` function (line 66) performs the actual permission check by calling `ac.Evaluate(ctx, user, injected)` (line 80). Access denial is handled by the `deny` function (line 87) which returns HTTP 403.\n\nPermission evaluation is driven by the `Evaluator` interface defined in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` (line 16), with the `EvalPermission` function (line 31) providing permission checks against action-scope pairs.\n\n## Folder and Dashboard Permission Inheritance\n\nThe permission inheritance chain is critical and implemented in `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`. The `GetInheritedScopes` function (line 196) retrieves parent folder scopes using `folderSvc.GetParents()` (line 203). Each dashboard inherits permissions from its parent folder through `resolveDashboardScope` (line 170), which:\n\n1. Gets the dashboard's folder UID or uses the General folder\n2. Calls GetInheritedScopes to get all ancestor folder scopes\n3. Returns scopes for dashboard, direct folder, and all parent folders\n\nScope resolution for dashboard access control is handled by `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver` (line 144) which converts scope references into actual permission scopes including inherited ones from parent folders. Similarly, `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver` (line 89) resolves folder scopes with parent folder inheritance.\n\n## Permission Services and API\n\nPermission management is exposed through two service interfaces in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go`: `FolderPermissionsService` and `DashboardPermissionsService`. These are implemented in the OSS access control module.\n\n`DashboardPermissionsService` in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` (line 82-186) configures permissions with:\n- PermissionsToActions mapping View/Edit/Admin to their respective action lists (lines 149-153)\n- InheritedScopesSolver that handles folder hierarchy (lines 121-142)\n- GetParentFolder callback for K8s API integration (lines 157-175)\n\n`FolderPermissionsService` in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` (line 91-156) similarly configures permissions with:\n- PermissionsToActions mapping View/Edit/Admin (lines 141-145)\n- InheritedScopesSolver using GetInheritedScopes (lines 131-134)\n\nAPI endpoints for managing these permissions are defined in:\n- `pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` for GET/POST dashboard permissions\n- `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` for GET/POST folder permissions\n\nThe three-tier model is strictly enforced: permissions cascade from parent to child folders (dashboard inherits folder permissions), and permission levels are hierarchical where Admin includes Edit which includes View permissions."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana User Permission Model Role Coverage Audit

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that define and enforce the three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin): the role-to-permission mapping, the permission guard middleware, and the folder/dashboard permission inheritance.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all the Go source files in grafana/grafana that define and enforce the three-tier permission model. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for relevant code.
Now let me create the comprehensive answer file with all the findings:

Perfect! I've successfully completed the audit and created a comprehensive answer file at `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings

I identified **14 critical Go source files** that define and enforce Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin):

### 1. **Role Definition Layer**
- `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` - Defines the RoleType with four constants (None, Viewer, Editor, Admin) and a precedence hierarchy
- `pkg/services/org/model.go` - Maps users to roles through OrgUser model

### 2. **Permission-to-Action Mapping**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` - Declares fixed roles and their grants via `DeclareFixedRoles`
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` - Maps dashboard permissions:
  - **View**: dashboards:read, annotations:read
  - **Edit**: adds dashboards:write, delete, annotations operations
  - **Admin**: adds dashboards.permissions:read/write
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` - Maps folder permissions with similar hierarchy plus alert and library panel management

### 3. **Permission Guard Middleware**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` - The `Middleware` function enforces permissions via the `Evaluator` interface
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` - Defines the `EvalPermission` function that checks actions against scopes

### 4. **Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance**
- `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` - Critical `GetInheritedScopes` function retrieves parent folder scopes, enabling hierarchical inheritance
- Scope resolvers (`NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver`, `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver`) convert resource references into permission scopes including parent folder inheritance

### 5. **API and Service Layer**
- `pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` and `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` - REST endpoints for permission management
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/` - Generic resource permission service framework

The implementation demonstrates a clean hierarchy where Admin permissions supersede Editor permissions, which supersede Viewer permissions, and all permissions cascade from parent folders to child resources.

arguments.query: repo:^github.com/grafana/grafana$ permission check middleware guard enforce
repo:^github.com/grafana/grafana$ role permission grant scope admin

The system is defined across multiple key files:\n\n## Core Role Definitions\n\nThe role hierarchy is defined in `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` with four role constants: RoleNone, RoleViewer, RoleEditor, and RoleAdmin. These roles have a precedence hierarchy (lines 18-23) where Admin > Editor > Viewer > None. The `pkg/services/org/model.go` defines the OrgUser model that associates users with these roles within organizations.\n\n## Permission Mapping and Enforcement\n\nRole-to-permission mapping is established in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` through the `DeclareFixedRoles` function (line 317), which grants specific fixed roles to each basic role. \n\nDashboard permissions are defined in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`:\n- DashboardViewActions (line 34) includes: dashboards:read, annotations:read\n- DashboardEditActions (line 35) adds: dashboards:write, dashboards:delete, annotations:write/delete/create\n- DashboardAdminActions (line 36) adds: dashboards.permissions:read and dashboards.permissions:write\n\nFolder permissions are defined in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`:\n- FolderViewActions (line 31) includes: folders:read, alerting:rules:read, library-panels:read, alerting:silences:read\n- FolderEditActions (line 32-44) adds write and delete actions plus dashboard creation and alert management\n- FolderAdminActions (line 45) adds: folders.permissions:read and folders.permissions:write\n\nEach role tier gets granted to the corresponding basic role through registerDashboardRoles (lines 43-79) and registerFolderRoles (lines 52-88), using the `Grants` field to specify which org roles receive each permission set.\n\n## Permission Guard Middleware\n\nThe permission enforcement middleware is implemented in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`. The `Middleware` function (line 30) creates a handler that evaluates permissions using the `Evaluator` interface. The `authorize` function (line 66) performs the actual permission check by calling `ac.Evaluate(ctx, user, injected)` (line 80). Access denial is handled by the `deny` function (line 87) which returns HTTP 403.\n\nPermission evaluation is driven by the `Evaluator` interface defined in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` (line 16), with the `EvalPermission` function (line 31) providing permission checks against action-scope pairs.\n\n## Folder and Dashboard Permission Inheritance\n\nThe permission inheritance chain is critical and implemented in `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`. The `GetInheritedScopes` function (line 196) retrieves parent folder scopes using `folderSvc.GetParents()` (line 203). Each dashboard inherits permissions from its parent folder through `resolveDashboardScope` (line 170), which:\n\n1. Gets the dashboard's folder UID or uses the General folder\n2. Calls GetInheritedScopes to get all ancestor folder scopes\n3. Returns scopes for dashboard, direct folder, and all parent folders\n\nScope resolution for dashboard access control is handled by `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver` (line 144) which converts scope references into actual permission scopes including inherited ones from parent folders. Similarly, `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver` (line 89) resolves folder scopes with parent folder inheritance.\n\n## Permission Services and API\n\nPermission management is exposed through two service interfaces in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go`: `FolderPermissionsService` and `DashboardPermissionsService`. These are implemented in the OSS access control module.\n\n`DashboardPermissionsService` in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` (line 82-186) configures permissions with:\n- PermissionsToActions mapping View/Edit/Admin to their respective action lists (lines 149-153)\n- InheritedScopesSolver that handles folder hierarchy (lines 121-142)\n- GetParentFolder callback for K8s API integration (lines 157-175)\n\n`FolderPermissionsService` in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` (line 91-156) similarly configures permissions with:\n- PermissionsToActions mapping View/Edit/Admin (lines 141-145)\n- InheritedScopesSolver using GetInheritedScopes (lines 131-134)\n\nAPI endpoints for managing these permissions are defined in:\n- `pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` for GET/POST dashboard permissions\n- `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` for GET/POST folder permissions\n\nThe three-tier model is strictly enforced: permissions cascade from parent to child folders (dashboard inherits folder permissions), and permission levels are hierarchical where Admin includes Edit which includes View permissions."
}
